planets.name: Social
planets.summary: Archive cluster around social: 3240 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Social receipt from 2018-08-19
artifacts.excerpt:
Nuclear weapons and cryptography have something in common. They both change the economics of conflict. 

Crypto makes it cheaper to defend than attack. 

Nuclear weapons make it economically viable to maintain a destructive arsenal during peacetime.

artifacts.title: Nostr receipt from 2022-12-19
artifacts.excerpt:
HOW TO KEEP TWITTER ALIVE

1. Fix Twitter Ads
2. Auction-off unused handles
3. Charge a small yearly fee for short handles (like domain names)
4. Lower the API pricing by ~95%
5. Keep Twitter max open by integrating Nostr

artifacts.title: Media receipt from 2020-11-17
artifacts.excerpt:
In hindsight, the model for disrupting media was simple:
 
1) Build a convenient, easy to use platform & handle recurring payments 
2) Let the authors own their audience (email lists) 
3) Don't try to decide what people are allowed to read 

The exact opposite of Medium.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: media, tech, mainstream, news.

artifacts.title: Media receipt from 2022-11-07
artifacts.excerpt:
History shows that when there is a step change in communication cost, a change in power structures follows, and the old power doesn't go without a fight.

(The Catholic church persecuted printers and destroyed their presses. The media has been vilifying Big Tech)
